fieldName: Show Flux Reticle Instead of Default Cursor
fieldDescription: Controls whether or not the default cursor is overridden. If false, only the vanilla cursor will be shown, but you will still be able to toggle "strafe and turn to cursor" behavior.

fieldName: Show Reticle While Interface is Hidden
fieldDescription: Controls whether or not the flux reticle is displayed instead of the cursor while the interface is hidden. The default cursor does not show up in screenshots, but the flux reticle does.
Changes the keybind for toggling "strafe and turn to cursor" behavior.
Scales the sprite parts of the flux reticle.
Scales only the front cursor graphic and glow as a multiplier of the regular reticle sprite size multiplier.
Selects which reticle sprite folder to use. All sets render at the same logical size; larger source images provide higher pixel density.
Selects an optional frontKeyTurn and frontMouseTurn wing-gap variant by folder name. CurrentSpriteSet uses the selected reticle sprite set.
Controls how visible the glow of the reticle is.
Moves only the front cursor graphic and glow along the ship-to-cursor line while the bar stays anchored. Positive values move it away from the ship; negative values move it toward the ship.
Moves only the front cursor graphic and glow left or right relative to the bar. Positive values move right when looking from the ship toward the cursor; negative values move left.
Moves the flux bar background, border, gauge fills, divider, and lower bar sprites left or right. Positive values move right when looking from the ship toward the cursor; negative values move left.
Draws the internal quarter middle and three-quarter divider marker sprites on the flux bar. The end cap sprite remains visible.

fieldName: Use Half Sprite For All Bar Markers
fieldDescription: Draws the half sprite at the one-quarter middle and three-quarter marker positions. When disabled the quarter and three-quarter positions use the quarter sprite and the middle position uses the half sprite.
Draws a second divider at the top of the soft flux fill using the same sprite color and size as the hard flux divider.
Controls the width of the flux bar drawn behind the reticle.
Controls the outline thickness around the flux bar background. The border uses the current reticle color; set to 0 to disable it.
Shrinks only the soft and hard flux fill quads inward from each side so the background border remains visible. Background and border size are unchanged.
Scales only the visual height/thickness of the hard and soft flux divider markers. Divider length and position are unchanged.
Controls the minimum drawn length of the flux bar.
Controls the maximum drawn length of the flux bar.
Ship-to-cursor distance where the bar is at minimum length. Distance is normalized to half the visible screen height.
Ship-to-cursor distance where the bar reaches maximum length. Distance is normalized to half the visible screen height.
Keeps the flux bar fully visible when the cursor is at or inside the minimum-size distance instead of fading it out at point blank range.
Draws a ship-system cooldown and ammo ring at a fixed local offset from the top of the flux bar.

fieldName: Fade Ship System Marker With Reticle
fieldDescription: Multiplies the ship-system marker opacity by the flux reticle fade amount.
Shows the remaining ship-system charge count in the center of the marker when the system uses charges.
Moves the ship-system marker left or right relative to the flux bar. Positive values move right when looking from the ship toward the cursor.
Moves the ship-system marker along the flux bar. Negative values move down the bar toward the ship; positive values move up past the top of the bar.
Controls the radius of the ship-system marker ring.
Controls the line thickness of the ship-system marker ring.
Scales the charge-count text drawn inside the ship-system marker.
Controls the stroke thickness of the charge-count digits inside the ship-system marker. This uses line rendering rather than a Starsector font asset.
Controls the opacity of the dynamic ship-system marker arc and charge text.
Controls the opacity of the full background ring behind the dynamic ship-system marker arc.
Flux level where warning flashing begins.
Flux level where warning flashing reaches full intensity.
Flash frequency in cycles per second when warning flashing begins.
Flash frequency in cycles per second at full warning intensity.
Allows the brief warning-color pulse caused by sudden flux level changes. Turn this off if rapidly unloading soft flux causes unwanted flashing.

defaultValue: Ship System Marker Out Of Ammo RGBA

System Marker Out Of Ammo Green
System Marker Out Of Ammo Blue
System Marker Out Of Ammo Alpha
